topActions.name: Adopt a Plant-Based Diet
topActions.description: Transition your diet away from high-emission animal products to vegetarian or vegan meals. This is the single most powerful dietary improvement you can make.

topActions.name: Use Public Transit or Carpool
topActions.description: Replace 3 days of weekly car trips with public transport, walking, or cycling. Saving is based on your daily travel of 45 km.

topActions.name: Shift to Circular & Second-hand Shopping
topActions.description: Transition to circular consumption: buy second-hand, rent clothing, or implement a 30-day cooling-off period before purchasing new items.

goal30.benefit: Reduce daily AC runtime by 2 hours (or set thermostat 2°C higher) to cut grid load during peak summer hours.

goal60.benefit: Replace 3 days of weekly car trips with public transport, walking, or cycling. Saving is based on your daily travel of 45 km.

goal90.benefit: Transition your diet away from high-emission animal products to vegetarian or vegan meals. This is the single most powerful dietary improvement you can make.

insights.coachSummary: Your diet is currently your leading source of emissions, accounting for 29% of your carbon footprint. Shifting from heavy meat meals to plant-based days will make a major, immediate dent in your output.

chatHistory.text: How can I reduce my transportation emissions?

I see you currently travel **45 km daily** using a **gasoline car**. This transportation habit produces **243 kg CO₂** per month, representing **28%** of your total footprint. 

Since transportation is a significant factor in your lifestyle, I recommend taking action: **Use Public Transit or Carpool**. By doing this, you could reduce your annual emissions by approximately **907 kg CO₂**! Shifting just 2 days of weekly travel to active transit (walking or cycling) or public transport is an excellent next step. You could also try joining our **No-Car Friday** challenge to build the habit.
